evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-boss', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-golem', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-celestial-events', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-equinox-worms', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-sisters-of-discord', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-ancients', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'pre-superancients', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: The Class Setups guide lists these items as obtainable choices at 'endgame', and every identifier resolves to an item in the current AAModClassic snapshot.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.11 snapshot recipe graph
evidence.reason: The current recipes combine Mining Shirt or Mining Pants with Stone Shell from Greed; the helmet already resolves through the same material at the Greed stage.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: The wiki identifies the set as craftable pre-Hardmode; the current recipes require Lucky Cracker from Samurai, first observed at Grips of Chaos in this snapshot.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: This is The Grips of Chaos expert treasure bag and contains Claw of Chaos.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 item namespaces
evidence.reason: The wiki identifies these as pre-Hardmode world, fishing, or biome items; their current snapshot types remain in pre-Hardmode content namespaces.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; Terraria 1.4.4.9; AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: These are Eye of Cthulhu, Blood Moon, or first Goblin Army rewards; the profile opens those optional early events at Eye of Cthulhu.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; AAModClassic 1.0.10 runtime snapshot
evidence.reason: Lucky Cracker is bought from Samurai, whose current runtime availability was first observed at the Grips of Chaos stage.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 Dungeon namespaces
evidence.reason: These loaded pre-Hardmode weapons belong to the Dungeon content namespace, which becomes normally accessible after Skeletron.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 removed-content recipes
evidence.reason: These legacy Mire and Hydra items are pre-Hardmode; the later required Hydra and Broodmother expert components are proven by the Queen Bee point in this profile.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 Hardmode namespaces
evidence.reason: The wiki and current namespaces identify these as Hardmode crafting, fishing, or ordinary-enemy items without a stated later boss gate.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot recipe graph
evidence.reason: The current recipe requires Storm Claw from Retriever in addition to earlier Grips of Chaos equipment.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot recipe graph
evidence.reason: The current recipe combines Storm Riot with Holo Cape from Raider Ultima and Storm Pendant from Orthrus X.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: These items require post-Plantera Terrarium, Jungle, Chlorophyte, or Solar Tablet progression.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 Martian Madness namespaces
evidence.reason: These are Martian Madness items, including Energy Conduit from Martian Saucer; the event opens after Golem.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10
evidence.reason: Romulus' Tazesaber is found in Greed's locked chests in the Cavern of the Worm King.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: Their current recipes require Dark Matter, Dark Energy, Radium, or Radiant Photon, all placed after The Equinox Worms by the guide.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipe
evidence.reason: The recipe requires Eventide Abyssium, which the guide places after Sisters of Discord.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: The current recipes require EX Soul from Shen Doragon Awakened.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot and final-boss support data
evidence.reason: Soul Stone is the final stone tied to the loaded Soul of Cthulhu content; Infinity Gauntlet requires it together with the other six stones.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot recipe graph
evidence.reason: The loaded recipe combines the four expert truffles, with Metallic Truffle from Techno Truffle as its latest ingredient.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 Anubis namespace
evidence.reason: This Hardmode special-edition Anubis weapon belongs to the Anubis content tier.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: The guide uses these stations for the post-Equinox material tier. Later recipes remain gated by their later ingredients, including EX Soul.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 recipes
evidence.reason: The guide uses this station for the post-Equinox material tier. Later recipes remain gated by their later ingredients, including EX Soul.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot source IDs
evidence.reason: The wiki classifies these as ordinary pre-Hardmode biome enemies or pre-Hardmode fishing/environment sources without a boss gate.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot source IDs
evidence.reason: The wiki classifies these as Hardmode biome enemies; no later boss gate is stated for their normal spawn tier.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot source IDs
evidence.reason: The crafting-material guide places Searing Spark and Terror Soul from these enemies after the Equinox Worms.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: All listed heads are segments of The Hydra encounter.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: Broodmini is listed as a boss servant and belongs to The Broodmother encounter.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: Both body IDs are variants of the Greed encounter.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: These are body and tail segments of Daybringer and Nightcrawler in The Equinox Worms encounter.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: These are normal and awakened body variants of the Akuma encounter already folded into one profile stage.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: The loaded legacy source is the unreleased variant of Anubis, Legendscribe.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9; AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: Goblin Slayer content follows the first Goblin Army, which this profile opens after Eye of Cthulhu.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: These shop ammunition items are gated by the calendar event, not boss progression.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: The profile begins repeatable pre-Hardmode Blood Moon progression after Eye of Cthulhu.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: Dreadnautilus is a Hardmode Blood Moon fishing enemy.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: The profile places this optional pre-Hardmode boss after Eye of Cthulhu.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: The Pirate Invasion is available in Hardmode.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: Pumpkin Moon is summoned with post-Plantera materials.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: Frost Moon is summoned with post-Plantera materials.

evidence.sourceVersion: Terraria 1.4.4.9
evidence.reason: Martian Madness becomes available after Golem.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot recipes
evidence.reason: These are world-generation blocks, fossils, or drops from ordinary pre-Hardmode biome objects and enemies.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: Terrarium enemies begin spawning after the Eater of Worlds or Brain of Cthulhu and drop Terra Shards.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 namespaces
evidence.reason: The wiki states that developer weapons and their grab bags come from Treasure Bags; these current item types are in the mod's Hardmode developer namespace.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; verified against AAModClassic 1.0.10 namespaces
evidence.reason: The wiki states that developer weapons and their grab bags come from Treasure Bags; these current item types are in the mod's PostMoonlord developer namespace.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot and runtime condition names
evidence.reason: The Infinity Stone branches explicitly require Post-Late Ancients; Zero is the last Late Ancient in this profile.

evidence.sourceVersion: AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: These conditions select a loot branch, time variant, or first-kill reward; the defeated NPC or opened boss bag remains the progression gate.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; checked against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: These old Class Setups identifiers do not resolve to any item in the current AAModClassic snapshot, so they must not be guessed or used to alter availability.

evidence.sourceVersion: Ancients Awakened 1.0.3.2 guide; checked against AAModClassic 1.0.10 snapshot
evidence.reason: Noodle Sword is explicitly listed by the weapons guide as unobtainable, and the current snapshot has no recipe, drop, shop, or fishing source.
